subject: Databases
title: DELETE Removes Rows
description: A quick win: understand DELETE Removes Rows.

hook.text: DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 42. The row is gone. No undo button unless you have a backup.

buildup.text: Like UPDATE, WHERE picks the target. Forget WHERE and you delete every single row.

discovery.text: Soft deletes add a deleted_at column instead of removing data. The row stays but is filtered from queries.

twist.text: Foreign keys can block deletes. Delete a customer who has orders? The database says no. Cascading deletes fix that.

climax.text: TRUNCATE is faster than DELETE for emptying a table. It resets the table without logging each row.

punchline.text: DELETE is permanent. Soft deletes give you an undo.

quiz.question: What is the advantage of soft deletes over hard deletes?
options: Data is preserved and can be recovered since the row is only marked, not removed
Soft deletes are faster than hard deletes
The database requires soft deletes for all tables
